Source URL: https://www.sba.gov/local-assistance

# SBDC vs SCORE vs SBA Routing Process

## SBDC is best for
- Local business development support.
- Local market knowledge.
- Structured business planning.
- Financial projections and funding-readiness preparation.
- Market research, competitor analysis, and local ecosystem navigation.
- Technical business development support tied to ZIP/county/service area.

## SCORE is best for
- One-on-one business mentoring.
- Operator judgment and strategic perspective.
- Ongoing accountability and mentor relationship.
- Broad industry mentor matching.

## SBA is best for
- Official education and program information.
- Funding program education.
- Federal contracting education and certification program information.
- Local assistance locator and resource-partner search.

## BOSS combined referral pattern
For early Houston entrepreneurs, it is often reasonable to recommend both SBDC and SCORE: SBDC for local business-development work and financial/market planning; SCORE for mentor judgment and accountability. Present the difference clearly so the user knows why each is useful.
